title: enkianthus subsessilis
intro: Enkianthus subsessilis, also known as the Korean alder or Korean fothergilla, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and red foliage in autumn. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Enkianthus subsessilis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Enkianthus subsessilis is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused into a tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
